Html:
I'm getting the following error when I attempt to attach the Commerce.mdf file to SQL Server Express2008. It appears that user 'JHSW500B\Joe Stagner' is the issue.

In SQL Server Management Studio I get the following error when trying to modify the schema owner:Cannot find the user 'JHSW500B\Joe Stagner', because it does not exist or you do not have permission.

 

Server Error in '/' Application.

Failed to generate a user instance of SQL Server due to failure in retrieving the user's local application data path. Please make sure the user has a local user profile on the computer. The connection will be closed.

Description: An unhandled exception occurred during the execution of the current web request. Please review the stack trace for more information about the error and where it originated in the code.

Exception Details: System.Data.SqlClient.SqlException: Failed to generate a user instance of SQL Server due to failure in retrieving the user's local application data path. Please make sure the user has a local user profile on the computer. The connection will be closed.

Line 36:                     RepeaterItemsList.DataBind();

I'm continuing to investigate this error and I'm now coming up with the following error:

I believe this is because I don't have account : 'JHSW500B\Joe Stagner'

I ended up getting the site to work by creating a new App Pool and setting the user to a SQL Server SysAdmin account. I made sure that that account was the DB Owner on the commerce database.
Check out my post under Commerce DB
